Preliminary view: the event is not material under UK MAR Article 7; no trading systems or order management platforms were affected; LSE ticker placeholder “AF00” and SEDOL “0000000” are used for illustration only.
A previously published RNS on 2 May announced the portal’s retirement; today’s incident does not change guidance and does not require a further RNS at this stage.
We reviewed two shared mailboxes—servicedesk@amberwickfinance.co.uk and deals@amberwickfinance.co.uk—and found no targeted spear‑phishing beyond generic spam.
Data rooms for ongoing M&A discussions are hosted on a segregated platform with separate credentials; logs show no access attempts and no downloads.
No accounts payable data, payroll records, or customer sort codes were accessed; strings resembling account numbers (e.g., 00-00-00/00000000) in logs are test placeholders.
The social‑media “leak” referenced a legacy sandbox and an invalid ISIN “XX0000000000”; these are non‑production artefacts not used for trading or disclosure.
We have notified the ICO via the standard breach webform as a precaution and will update the FCA Market Watch contact point if any MAR‑relevant facts emerge; currently none do.
